evals.prompt: I've got about $250 AUD and I've never built anything electronic before. My 10 year old and I want to build a robot we can drive around the house with a controller, and maybe program later. What should we buy?
evals.expected_output: Profiles budget/skill/goal, recommends a T0/T1 tier build with specific parts (kit or Pi rover, gamepad, batteries, tools), warns about LiPo safety, reserves budget for sundries, offers an upgrade path to programmability.

evals.prompt: I have a Jetson Orin Nano and a RealSense D435i. I want my rover to follow me around the backyard. It already drives via teleop. Walk me through it.
evals.expected_output: TensorRT-exported YOLO person detection + tracking, depth-based range/bearing, follow controller publishing velocity goals behind a watchdog/safety layer, warns about IR depth outdoors in sunlight, staged testing (on blocks first).

evals.prompt: How do I check on my robot when I'm away from home? I was going to forward port 22 on my router to the Pi.
evals.expected_output: Refuses port-forwarding as the approach, recommends Tailscale/WireGuard overlay VPN, SSH key hardening, firewall default-deny, and isolated IoT network; explains why exposed robots are scanned/compromised.

evals.prompt: My two-wheel balancing robot oscillates violently and falls over after about 2 seconds. ESP32, MPU6050, TB6612, 200Hz loop, complementary filter, single PID on tilt angle. Gains Kp=40 Ki=5 Kd=0.5.
evals.expected_output: Routes to control-and-stability.md: checks loop-rate jitter and dt handling, suggests integral windup clamp / drop Ki initially, raise Kd relative to Kp, verify filter coefficient and IMU mounting/vibration, motor deadband feedforward, battery-voltage scaling; recommends tuning sequence Kp then Kd then tiny Ki, one gain at a time with logged plots.

evals.prompt: I want my quadruped to learn to walk. I have a gaming PC with an RTX 4080. Where do I start?
evals.expected_output: Routes to simulation-and-gyms.md + control-and-stability.md: RL in simulation (Isaac Lab with shipped quadruped example tasks as templates), Gymnasium mechanics first via CartPole/stable-baselines3, domain randomization and sim2real checklist, honest actuator-cost realities for real hardware, deploy behind deterministic safety layer.
